entries.title: C1: updated_at Override Bug in Upsert Operations
entries.content:
CRITICAL data integrity bug in qdrant-client.ts upsert/batchUpsert methods.

Bug: Payload construction spreads metadata after setting updated_at, causing metadata.updated_at to override the fresh timestamp. When reindexing existing memories, the original creation timestamp is preserved instead of being updated.

Affected lines:
- qdrant-client.ts upsert: lines 354-365
- qdrant-client.ts batchUpsert: lines 412-423

Root cause: Spread operator order. When metadata is spread after updated_at assignment, it overwrites updated_at with stale value from existing.metadata.

Impact:
- Corrupts sort-by-updated_at queries
- Modified memories appear ancient in recency-sorted results
- Long-term timeline integrity compromised
- Breaks expected behavior of memory-update tool with reindex=true
- Memory modification times become unreliable

Solution (preferred): Delete updated_at from metadata before spread:
const metadata = { ...inputMetadata };
delete metadata.updated_at;
const payload = { ..., updated_at: now, ...metadata };

Alternative solution: Move assignment after spread:
const payload = { ..., ...metadata, updated_at: now };

Regression testing required:
1. Memory's updated_at changes to current time after update
2. Queries ordering by updated_at return correct chronological sort order
3. Batch update operations set fresh timestamp on all records
4. Chunk metadata preserves chunk_group_id but updates updated_at
5. Verify backward compatibility with existing stored memories

Related to: C2 (testing gaps), memory-update tool behavior

Priority: URGENT - fix before release
Estimated effort: 2-3 hours including regression tests

entries.title: C2: Coverage Thresholds Not Enforced in CI
entries.content:
CRITICAL quality gate bypass: CI runs 'yarn test' instead of 'yarn test:coverage', so 80% coverage thresholds are never validated or enforced.

Actual coverage measured April 24, 2026:
- Statements: 70.46% (required: 80%) ❌ FAIL by 9.54%
- Functions: 62.83% (required: 80%) ❌ FAIL by 17.17%
- Branches: 54.38% (required: 80%) ❌ FAIL by 25.62%
- Lines: 71.80% (required: 80%) ❌ FAIL by 8.20%

Critical coverage gaps - qdrant-client.ts at only 22% function/branch coverage:
Untested paths:
- batchUpsert() — batch update operations not exercised
- hybridSearchWithRRF() — RRF ranking and fusion logic untested
- validateCollectionSchema() — collection schema validation path uncovered
- close() — connection cleanup and teardown untested
- Other critical paths with missing test scenarios

Other modules: Multiple services below 80% threshold (embedding-service, rules-manager, workspace-detector)

Root cause: CI configuration runs standard test suite without coverage checking. Vitest configured with 80% threshold but CI doesn't invoke it.

Files requiring modification:
1. .github/workflows/ci.yml — Change test step from 'yarn test' to 'yarn test:coverage'
2. .husky/pre-commit — Consider adding coverage check (optional; can be CI-only enforcement)
3. src/services/__tests__/qdrant-client.spec.ts — Add missing integration tests
4. Other test files — Fill remaining gaps to reach 80%

Implementation plan (once enabled, coverage will fail immediately):
Phase 1 (4-6 hours): Add qdrant-client.ts tests
- batchUpsert with multiple items and mixed metadata
- hybridSearchWithRRF with various query scenarios and RRF combinations
- validateCollectionSchema with valid/invalid schemas and edge cases
- close() and reconnection scenarios

Phase 2 (2-3 hours): Fix remaining gaps
- embedding-service tests
- rules-manager tests
- workspace-detector tests
- config tests

Expected outcome: All 4 metrics ≥80% on all modules

Related to: C1 (missing regression tests), overall code quality

Priority: URGENT - quality gates critical for release
Estimated effort: 6-8 hours total for full remediation

entries.title: C3: README Documents Removed Features (Local Embeddings)
entries.content:
CRITICAL documentation issue: README.md and .env.example still reference local/ONNX embeddings that were removed in April 2026 migration.

Removed features still documented:
- EMBEDDING_PROVIDER configuration variable
- LOCAL_EMBEDDING_MODEL configuration variable
- EMBEDDING_DIMENSION configuration variable
- Examples showing local embedding setup

Misleading documentation:
- OPENAI_API_KEY listed as optional, but now REQUIRED after April 2026 migration
- No migration note explaining breaking change
- No clear statement that local embeddings no longer supported

Impact on users:
- Users attempt to configure local embeddings and receive configuration errors
- Confusion about what configuration options are valid
- Debugging difficulty when setup fails with undocumented requirements
- Trust in documentation decreases
- Migration path from previous version unclear

Files requiring updates:
1. README.md
   - Prerequisites section: Remove all local embedding configuration details
   - Configuration section: Remove EMBEDDING_PROVIDER, LOCAL_EMBEDDING_MODEL, EMBEDDING_DIMENSION
   - Add clear statement: "OpenAI API key is required. Local embeddings are not supported."
   - Update quick-start examples to show only OpenAI configuration
   - Add migration note: "Local embeddings were removed in April 2026. Use OpenAI embeddings instead."

2. .env.example
   - Remove all local embedding variables
   - Keep only OpenAI-related env vars
   - Mark OPENAI_API_KEY as required

3. AGENTS.md (if it references local embeddings)
   - Verify no outdated local embedding references

Implementation checklist:
- [ ] Review and update README.md (remove local embedding docs)
- [ ] Remove EMBEDDING_PROVIDER, LOCAL_EMBEDDING_MODEL, EMBEDDING_DIMENSION from README
- [ ] Update .env.example (remove embedding provider variables)
- [ ] Clearly mark OPENAI_API_KEY as REQUIRED in prerequisites
- [ ] Add migration note documenting April 2026 breaking change
- [ ] Verify all documented environment variables are still supported
- [ ] Update all configuration examples to show OpenAI setup only
- [ ] Review and test configuration examples with clean environment
- [ ] Update any internal docs or wiki that reference embeddings configuration

Priority: URGENT - misleading users about requirements
Estimated effort: 2-3 hours for all updates

entries.title: C4: memory-update Silently Discards Content When reindex=false
entries.content:
CRITICAL API design issue: memory-update tool silently ignores the 'content' parameter when reindex=false (the default), returning success with no error indication.

Problematic behavior:
Client request:
{
  "id": "memory-123",
  "content": "New content here",
  "reindex": false
}

Server response:
{
  "success": true,
  "reindexed": false
}

Result: Client receives success response but the supplied content is dropped. The embedding and stored content remain unchanged.

Root cause (memory-tools.ts memoryUpdateHandler):
- When reindex=false, content parameter is NOT merged into the update payload
- No validation that if content is provided, reindex must be true
- No error or warning returned to client
- Success response is returned regardless of whether content was actually updated

Impact on users:
- Silent data loss: clients believe they updated memory content but didn't
- No error to alert developers during debugging
- API contract violation: parameters passed but ignored
- Inconsistent state: request implies update but operation does nothing
- Difficult to diagnose: success response masks the actual failure
- Users unknowingly storing stale content

Root cause analysis:
The tool schema allows both 'content' and 'reindex=false' together, but the implementation doesn't validate this combination. This is a classic footgun design pattern.

Recommended solution (Option A - simpler, preferred):
Default reindex=true when content is provided. Automatically determine reindex behavior based on input.

Implementation:
async function memoryUpdateHandler(args: unknown) {
  const input = MemoryUpdateInputSchema.parse(args);
  const shouldReindex = input.content !== undefined; // Auto-reindex if content given
  // ... rest of handler using shouldReindex instead of input.reindex
}

Pros:
- Simple and intuitive API
- Removes confusing parameter combination
- Prevents accidental data loss
- Still allows metadata-only updates (when content is undefined)

Cons:
- Slight behavior change from explicit reindex parameter
- May surprise some clients expecting explicit control

Alternative solution (Option B - explicit error):
Validate and error on invalid combination. Return a validation error when content is provided with reindex=false.

if (input.content && !input.reindex) {
  return validationError(
    'Cannot provide content without reindex: set reindex=true',
    'INVALID_PARAMETER_COMBINATION',
    ...
  );
}

Pros:
- Explicit error catches bugs early
- Maintains backward compatibility with explicit semantics

Cons:
- Breaking change for existing clients
- More verbose error handling
- Clients must update their code

Recommendation: Implement Option A for simpler API and better user experience.

Regression testing required:
1. Update with content and reindex=true → content changed, embedding updated
2. Update with content and reindex=false → content changed, embedding updated (Option A)
3. Update without content → content unchanged
4. Update metadata without content → metadata changed, content unchanged
5. Verify all existing memories still queryable and sorted correctly
6. Edge case: empty string content → treated as valid content update
7. Edge case: content with special characters → properly indexed and embedded

Related to: C1 (timestamp issues), memory-update tool contract, API design

Priority: URGENT - potential data loss
Estimated effort: 3-4 hours (decision, implementation, comprehensive testing)

entries.title: High-Severity Issues: Type Safety, Testing, Documentation
entries.content:
Multiple high-severity issues across type safety, test coverage, and documentation accuracy.

=== H1: Unsafe Type Cast in validateCollectionSchema ===

Severity: HIGH (type safety violation)
File: src/services/qdrant-client.ts, line 205
Issue: Violates pawells TypeScript standards

Current code (❌ WRONG):
response.result.config as Record<string, unknown>

Correct code (✓ PROPER):
response.result.config as unknown as Record<string, unknown>

Violation: Direct type assertion without unknown intermediate. Pawells standards require casting through 'unknown' for type safety.

Impact: Reduces type safety, bypasses TypeScript's cast validation
Fix: Add 'unknown' intermediate in cast chain
Effort: 0.5 hours
Priority: HIGH

=== H2: Missing Regression Test for updated_at Override (C1) ===

Severity: HIGH (test coverage gap)
File: src/services/__tests__/qdrant-service.spec.ts
Issue: Existing test doesn't catch C1 bug

Existing test named "sets created_at and updated_at timestamps" verifies that timestamps are set, but does NOT verify that provided metadata.updated_at gets overridden with fresh timestamp during upsert.

Problem: This test would still pass even with the C1 bug present, because the bug is specifically about REINDEX operations preserving old timestamps.

Missing test scenarios:
1. Store memory with custom timestamp
2. Update memory with reindex=true
3. Verify updated_at changed to current time
4. Verify timestamps correctly ordered in sort query

This test gap allowed C1 bug to go undetected. Once C1 is fixed, add regression test to prevent recurrence.

Impact: Allows data integrity bugs to be deployed
Fix: Add comprehensive timestamp lifecycle tests
Effort: 2-3 hours
Priority: HIGH (tied to C1 and C2)

=== H3: Hybrid Search Documentation Inaccurate ===

Severity: HIGH (documentation)
File: AGENTS.md, Architecture section
Issue: Wrong search technology description

Documented (❌ WRONG):
"Supports dense HNSW + sparse BM25 hybrid search"

Actual implementation:
"Supports dense HNSW embeddings + keyword full-text index with Reciprocal Rank Fusion (RRF) for hybrid search"

Correct description should be:
- Dense vectors: HNSW (Hierarchical Navigable Small World)
- Sparse index: Qdrant keyword full-text index (NOT BM25)
- Fusion: Reciprocal Rank Fusion (RRF) with configurable alpha parameter

Impact: Users misunderstand ranking mechanism and hybrid search capability
Fix: Update AGENTS.md Architecture section with correct details
Effort: 1 hour
Priority: HIGH

=== H4: Config Type Cast Robustness (Medium Priority) ===

Severity: MEDIUM
File: src/config.ts, line 175
Issue: Type coercion happens before Zod validation

Current approach: Type cast first, then validate
Better approach: Let Zod handle coercion, validate result

This is lower priority than critical issues but affects config robustness.

Estimated effort: 1 hour
Priority: MEDIUM

entries.title: mcp-memory April 2026 Code Review - Release Status & Recommendations
entries.content:
Comprehensive code quality review of @pawells/mcp-memory completed April 24, 2026.

=== RELEASE STATUS: 🔴 BLOCKED ===

Four critical issues prevent release to npm. Must be resolved before publishing.

Review date: April 24, 2026
Reviewer: Automated code quality audit
Statement coverage measured: 70.46% (required: 80%)
Functions coverage measured: 62.83% (required: 80%)
Branches coverage measured: 54.38% (required: 80%)
Lines coverage measured: 71.80% (required: 80%)

=== CRITICAL ISSUES BLOCKING RELEASE ===

1. C1: updated_at override bug — Corrupts memory timestamps, breaks sort-by-recency queries
2. C2: Coverage thresholds not enforced in CI — Quality gates bypassed, low-coverage code deployed
3. C3: README documents removed features — Misleads users about configuration requirements
4. C4: memory-update silently discards content — Silent data loss when reindex=false

Estimated effort to fix all critical: 14-21 hours

=== CODE QUALITY ASSESSMENT ===

Strengths (well-executed):
✅ Excellent import organization (4-group structure, import type, .js extensions)
✅ Strong type safety (strict mode, null vs undefined distinction, no any types)
✅ Comprehensive error handling (custom error classes, proper cause chains)
✅ Complete JSDoc documentation (all exported symbols, @param -, @throws, @example)
✅ Proper async/await patterns (no .then() chains, Promise.all usage, no swallowed errors)
✅ Configuration full compliance (Zod validation, env vars, 4 tsconfig files)
✅ CI/CD full compliance (frozen lockfile, HUSKY=0, proper workflow structure)
✅ ESLint & Prettier: Properly configured v10+ flat config
✅ Node.js version policy: >=22.0.0, .nvmrc, engines field correct
✅ Git hooks: Husky 9 configured with pre-commit lint + typecheck

Weaknesses (require attention):
❌ Coverage gaps: Actual 54-71%, required 80%; qdrant-client.ts 22% function/branch
❌ API design: Silent failures (memory-update ignores content parameter)
❌ Documentation: Lag post-April 2026 migration (removed features still documented)
❌ Type assertions: Some bypassing unknown intermediate (line 205 qdrant-client.ts)
❌ Testing: Missing integration tests for batch operations and hybrid search RRF logic

=== RELEASE CHECKLIST ===

Before publishing v1.x.x to npm:

[ ] C1 FIXED: updated_at override bug corrected in upsert/batchUpsert
[ ] C1 TESTED: Regression test added for timestamp lifecycle
[ ] C2 ENABLED: CI test step changed to yarn test:coverage
[ ] C2 FIXED: qdrant-client.ts coverage increased to >=80% on all metrics
[ ] C2 VERIFIED: All modules reach 80% statements, functions, branches, lines
[ ] C3 FIXED: README.md updated, local embedding docs removed
[ ] C3 FIXED: .env.example cleaned of removed variables
[ ] C3 FIXED: OPENAI_API_KEY marked as REQUIRED
[ ] C3 FIXED: Migration note added for April 2026 breaking change
[ ] C4 RESOLVED: memory-update API behavior fixed or documented
[ ] H1 FIXED: AGENTS.md hybrid search description corrected (keyword full-text + RRF)
[ ] H2 FIXED: validateCollectionSchema type cast corrected (as unknown as Record)
[ ] COVERAGE VERIFIED: yarn test:coverage shows >=80% on all 4 metrics, all modules
[ ] LINT PASSED: yarn lint returns zero errors
[ ] TYPECHECK PASSED: yarn typecheck returns zero errors
[ ] BUILD PASSED: yarn build produces clean output, no warnings
[ ] TESTS PASSED: yarn test:coverage fully passing
[ ] CHANGELOG UPDATED: All fixes, breaking changes, and deprecations documented
[ ] VERSION BUMPED: package.json version updated per semver
[ ] TAG CREATED: v<semver> tag created and pushed to trigger publish workflow
[ ] GITHUB RELEASE: Auto-created from tag with changelog
[ ] NPM PUBLISHED: Package published with provenance, files array correct

=== NEXT ACTIONS ===

Immediate (this sprint):
1. Fix C1: updated_at override bug (2-3 hours)
2. Fix C4: Decide memory-update approach and implement (3-4 hours)
3. Fix C3: Update documentation (2-3 hours)
4. Enable C2: Coverage in CI (1 hour)

Following sprint:
5. Add test coverage for qdrant-client.ts and other modules (6-8 hours)
6. Fix H1, H2: Documentation and type safety (1-2 hours)
7. Verify all checklist items
8. Prepare release

=== TECHNICAL DEBT ===

Post-release considerations:
- Integration test timeouts (minor)
- Error handling gaps in edge cases (minor)
- Logging inconsistencies across services (minor)
- Performance optimization for large result sets
- Memory optimization for batch operations

=== RELEASE NOTES TEMPLATE ===

When ready to release, note:
- BREAKING: Local embeddings removed; OPENAI_API_KEY now required
- FIXED: Data integrity issue with updated_at timestamps
- FIXED: memory-update behavior with content parameter
- IMPROVED: Coverage increased to 80%+ on all metrics
- IMPROVED: Documentation accuracy for hybrid search
- See CHANGELOG for full details

=== CONFIDENCE & VALIDATION ===

All findings validated via:
- Direct code inspection (CRITICAL)
- Coverage reports from yarn test:coverage
- Configuration file analysis
- Documentation review
- API schema validation

Confidence: 0.95 (based on measurable metrics and direct code inspection)

Related memories:
- C1: updated_at_override_bug.md
- C2: coverage_enforcement_disabled.md
- C3: documentation_updates_needed.md
- C4: silent_content_discard.md
- H1-H2: additional_issues.md

instructions: These 6 memory entries represent the comprehensive code review findings for mcp-memory. Each entry should be stored using the mcp-memory MCP server's memory-store tool with the specified metadata. All entries are marked as long-term memories. Total estimated effort to address all critical issues: 14-21 hours. Release is blocked until all C1-C4 issues are resolved.
